message:
feat(widgets): wire IME composition into TextInput and add host-focus lifecycle to FocusManager

Three coordinated widget-layer changes that complete the IME event
pipeline from core through to user-facing widgets and ensure the focus
graph responds correctly to host window focus transitions.

TextInput IME handling (input.rs):
  - handle_event() now matches Event::Ime and delegates to a new
    handle_ime_event() method that dispatches on ImePhase:
    - Start: calls ime_start_composition() to begin tracking.
    - Update: calls ime_update_composition() with preedit text.
    - Commit: if an active composition exists, updates then commits;
      otherwise inserts text directly (standalone commit without
      preceding start, as some IME implementations may do).
    - Cancel: calls ime_cancel_composition() to restore pre-IME state.
  - operation_name() returns phase-specific strings (ime_start,
    ime_update, ime_commit, ime_cancel) for action timeline display.
  - Two new tests: handle_event_ime_update_and_commit verifies the
    full start->update->commit lifecycle inserts text at the cursor;
    handle_event_ime_cancel verifies rollback to original text.

FocusManager host-focus lifecycle (focus/manager.rs):
  - New apply_host_focus(focused: bool) method implements a
    deterministic policy for translating host/window focus events
    into widget focus graph mutations:
    - focus lost: unconditionally blurs (clears current focus).
    - focus gained: if a valid focused node exists and is allowed by
      any active trap, preserves it (no-op). Otherwise, restores
      focus to the first node in the active trap group, or the first
      node in the graph if no trap is active.
  - Returns bool indicating whether focus state actually changed.
  - Four new tests: loss blurs current, gain focuses first when
    unfocused, gain preserves valid current, gain respects trap
    ordering.

FocusAwareModalStack integration (modal/focus_integration.rs):
  - handle_event() now intercepts Event::Focus and delegates to
    apply_host_focus() before forwarding to the modal stack, ensuring
    focus traps are correctly maintained across Alt-Tab cycles.
  - Two new tests: focus loss blurs current, focus gain restores
    trapped focus after blur.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
